mapper.select:
select p.id, p.user_id, p.item_id, p.payment_state, i.item_name, i.item_content, i.item_price, s.store_name, m.market_name, p.created_datetime from tbl_payment p join tbl_item i on p.item_id = i.id join tbl_store s on i.item_store_id = s.id join tbl_market m on s.store_market_id = m.id where p.user_id = #{userId} and p.payment_state = 'complete'
limit #{criteria.count} offset #{criteria.offset}

select p.id, p.user_id, p.item_id, p.payment_state, i.item_name, i.item_content, i.item_price, s.store_name, m.market_name, p.created_datetime from tbl_payment p join tbl_item i on p.item_id = i.id join tbl_store s on i.item_store_id = s.id join tbl_market m on s.store_market_id = m.id where p.user_id = #{userId} and p.payment_state = 'pending'
limit #{criteria.count} offset #{criteria.offset}
select count(*) from tbl_payment where user_id = #{userId} and payment_state = 'complete'
select count(*) from tbl_payment where user_id = #{userId} and payment_state = 'pending'

select p.id, p.user_id, p.item_id, p.payment_state, i.item_name, i.item_price, p.created_datetime from tbl_payment p join tbl_item i on p.item_id = i.id join tbl_store s on i.item_store_id = s.id where s.store_owner_id = #{userId} order by p.created_datetime desc

select p.id, p.user_id, p.item_id, p.payment_state, p.created_datetime, i.item_name, i.item_content, i.item_price, i.item_delivery_fee, s.store_name, m.market_name, u.user_name, u.user_phone, u.user_email from tbl_payment p join tbl_item i on p.item_id = i.id join tbl_store s on i.item_store_id = s.id join tbl_market m on s.store_market_id = m.id join tbl_user u on p.user_id = u.id where p.id = #{id}
